title: Sputum symptom documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports sputum amount, colour and associated cough history for sputum symptom documentation; no decision is generated.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Specifies cough duration, quality, timing, risk factors, and associated-symptom history domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

red_flags.text: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician-assessed cough warning prompts for sputum symptom documentation; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists cough features that require serious-condition assessment or face-to-face escalation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Cough – 46

follow_up.text: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.
follow_up.rationale: Supports sputum change within COPD exacerbation features for sputum symptom documentation; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

source.title: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease in over 16s: diagnosis and management

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Onset, amount, colour, consistency, odour, blood, fever, breathlessness, chest pain, smoking, examination, tests, diagnosis and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for sputum symptom documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
